name: James v. Kentucky
summary: Right to remain silent and have silence not used against defendant; presumption of innocence instruction must be given when requested and supported by evidence.
holding: Due process violated when trial court refuses to give presumption of innocence instruction that accurately states law and is supported by evidence.
constitutional_basis: Fifth Amendment Due Process, Fourteenth Amendment
relevance_to_audit: Relevant to administrative hearing procedures and presumptions in enforcement actions; establishes requirements for neutral adjudication.
